tool.name: Trackastra track
tool.description: link segmented objects across a microscopy time series
tool.command:
mkdir -p 'ctc_output' 'home' && export HOME="\$PWD/home" && export OMP_NUM_THREADS="\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}" && export MKL_NUM_THREADS="\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}" && #set $images_path = 'images' #set $masks_path = 'masks'
#if $inputs.input_format == 'collections': mkdir -p 'images' 'masks' && #for $i, $image in enumerate($inputs.images): #set $frame = '%04d' % $i ln -s '$image' 'images/image_${frame}.tif' && #end for #for $i, $mask in enumerate($inputs.masks): #set $frame = '%04d' % $i ln -s '$mask' 'masks/mask_${frame}.tif' && #end for image_count=\$(find 'images' -maxdepth 1 -type l | wc -l) && mask_count=\$(find 'masks' -maxdepth 1 -type l | wc -l) && if [ "\$image_count" -ne "\$mask_count" ]; then echo 'The image and mask collections must contain the same number of elements.' >&2; exit 1; fi && #else: ln -s '$inputs.images_stack' 'images_stack.tif' && ln -s '$inputs.masks_stack' 'masks_stack.tif' && #set $images_path = 'images_stack.tif' #set $masks_path = 'masks_stack.tif' #end if
#if $model_source.source == 'cached': #set $model_path = $model_source.model.fields.path #else: mkdir -p 'custom_model' && ln -s '$model_source.model_weights' 'custom_model/model.pt' && ln -s '$model_source.architecture_config' 'custom_model/config.yaml' && ln -s '$model_source.training_config' 'custom_model/train_config.yaml' && #set $model_path = 'custom_model' #end if
trackastra track --imgs '$images_path' --masks '$masks_path' --output-ctc 'ctc_output' --output-edge-table 'edge_table.csv' --model-custom '$model_path' --mode '$mode' --max-distance '$max_distance'

param.name: input_format
param.label: Input organization
param.option: Ordered collections: one TIFF per time point

param.name: images
param.label: Microscopy images
param.help: Ordered list collection containing one image TIFF per time point. List order defines time.

param.name: masks
param.label: Instance-segmentation masks
param.help: Ordered list collection containing one integer-labelled mask TIFF per time point. The number and order of elements must match the image collection.

param.name: masks_stack
param.label: Instance-segmentation mask time series
param.help: Integer-labelled multi-page TIFF with shape T,(Z),Y,X, matching the image time series.

param.name: source
param.label: Trackastra model source
param.option: Model installed on this Galaxy server

param.name: model
param.label: Installed Trackastra model
param.help: Models are installed by a Galaxy administrator using the Trackastra model data manager.

validator.message: No Trackastra models are installed on this Galaxy server. Ask an administrator to run the Trackastra model data manager, or select model components from history.

param.name: model_weights
param.label: Model weights (model.pt)
param.help: Trackastra model state dictionary. Use only a model from a trusted source; Trackastra loads it with PyTorch's weights-only mode.

param.label: Maximum linking distance
param.help: Maximum spatial distance, in pixels or voxels, considered while constructing candidate links.

has_text.text: must contain the same number of elements

tool.help:
**Trackastra** associates objects that have already been segmented in a microscopy time series. It does not perform segmentation.
Input organization ==================
Use either two ordered Galaxy list collections or two multi-page TIFF datasets. Images and masks must have matching time points and spatial dimensions. Masks must be integer-labelled instance segmentations; background is label 0.
Models ======
For reproducible execution, pretrained models are installed once by a Galaxy administrator with the accompanying Trackastra model data manager. This avoids downloading model files during ordinary analysis jobs. A complete custom model can instead be supplied from history as ``model.pt``, ``config.yaml``, and ``train_config.yaml``.
Trackastra loads ``model.pt`` with PyTorch ``weights_only=True``. Nevertheless, use custom model files only when their origin is trusted.
Linking modes =============
- **greedy**: fast linking that permits cell divisions. - **greedy_nodiv**: fast linking without divisions. - **ilp**: integer-linear-programming linking. This is slower and depends on a solver available in the Trackastra environment.
Resources =========
The wrapper sets common numerical-library thread limits from the CPU allocation selected by the Galaxy job destination through ``GALAXY_SLOTS``. Trackastra's feature extraction worker count and inference batch size use their upstream defaults. CPU/GPU placement is intentionally not exposed as a user parameter; Trackastra automatically uses CUDA when the administrator routes the job to a compatible GPU destination.
Outputs =======
- **Association edge table**: CSV containing source/target frame and label identifiers plus association weights. - **CTC track table**: space-delimited ``track_id start_frame end_frame parent_track_id`` records. - **Tracked masks**: an ordered collection of relabelled TIFF masks in Cell Tracking Challenge form.
